steps.title: Substitute the isolated equation into the second equation
steps.content:
The first equation already isolates $y$. Substitute $y = 6x + 16$ into the second equation $-7x - y = 36$:

$$-7x - (6x + 16) = 36$$

steps.title: Simplify and solve for x
steps.content:
Expand the left side and combine like terms:
$$-7x - 6x - 16 = 36$$
$$-13x - 16 = 36$$
$$-13x = 52$$
$$x = -4$$

steps.title: Find y and confirm the solution
steps.content:
Substitute $x = -4$ back into the first equation $y = 6x + 16$:
$$y = 6(-4) + 16 = -24 + 16 = -8$$
The solution is $(-4, -8)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
